title: A cone assembly and method of manufacturing such assembly
abstract: A cone assembly for furniture or interior accessories is provided. A plurality of cones (C1, C2) are arranged in a linear array to form the cone assembly (40), wherein an inner cone (C1) is received by at least one outer cone (C2); and wherein the outer cone (C2) is made of a first layer (L1) of material being different from the material of the inner cone (C1)
claims: Claims 1. A cone assembly for furniture or interior accessories, comprising: a plurality of cones (Cl, C2) arranged in a linear array to form the cone assembly (40), wherein an inner cone (Cl) is received by at least one outer cone (C2); and wherein the outer cone (C2) is made of a first layer (Ll) of material being different from the material of the inner cone (Cl). 2. The cone assembly according to claim 1, wherein the length of the outer cone (C2) and the length of the inner cone (Cl) are both substantially equal to the length of the cone assembly. 3. The cone assembly according to any of the preceding claims, wherein at least the outer cone (C2) is formed by attaching the longitudinal edges (8) of the first layer (Ll) of material to each other. 4. The cone assembly according to any of claims 1 to 3, wherein the inner cone (Cl) has a uniform thickness at each specific longitudinal position. 5. The cone assembly according to any of claims 1-4, further comprising at least one intermediate cone (C3) arranged between the inner cone (Cl) and the outer cone (C2). 6. The cone assembly according to claim 5, wherein at least one of the intermediate cones (C3) is made of a material being the same, or different from the material of the inner cone (Cl). 7. The cone assembly according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the outer cone (C2) and/or at least one of the intermediate cones (C3) is formed by a first layer (Ll) of material arranged adjacent to a second layer (L2) of material, the material of the second layer (L2) being different from the material of the first layer (Ll). 8. The cone assembly according to claim 7, wherein the second layer (L2) is made of a backing material. 9. The cone assembly according to claim 8, wherein the backing material is a paper-based material. 10. The cone assembly according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the first layer (Ll) of material comprises one or more of the following materials: wood veneer, cork, paper, hemp, cellulose, laminate, thermosetting resin impregnated paper, thermoplastic sheet, metal, or medium density fiberboard (MDF). 11. The cone assembly according to claim 3, wherein the longitudinal edges (8) of the first layer (Ll) of material are attached to each other by i) providing the edges (8) with an adhesive prior to arranging the cone (C2) in the linear array, ii) allowing adhesive to flow along the longitudinal edges (8) as a result of an inner cone (Cl, C3), having the adhesive applied on its outer surface, being arranged inside the outer cone (C2), and/or iii) allowing an adhesive surface coating to flow along the longitudinal edges (8) as a result of the outer surface of the outer cone (C2) being covered by said surface coating. 12. The cone assembly according to claim 3 or 11, wherein the inner cone (Cl) and/or any of the intermediate cones (C3) is formed by attaching longitudinal edges (8) of a material to each other. 13. The cone assembly according to claim 12, wherein the joint between the longitudinal edges (8) of the outer cone (C2) is spaced apart, in the circumferential direction, from the joint between the longitudinal edges (8) of the inner cone (Cl) and/or the joint between the longitudinal edges (8) of at least one of the intermediate cones (C3). 14. The cone assembly according to claim 12 or 13, wherein the joint between the longitudinal edges of a cone (Cl, C2, C3) is straight. 15. The cone assembly according to claim 12 or 13, wherein the joint between the longitudinal edges of a cone (Cl, C2, C3) is curved. 16. The cone assembly according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the at least one outer cone (C2) is hollow. 17. The cone assembly according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the innermost cone (Cl) is hollow. 18. The cone assembly according to any of claims 1-16, wherein the innermost cone (Cl) is solid. 19. The cone assembly according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the innermost cone (Cl) extends along the entire length of the at least one outer cone (C2). 20. The cone assembly according to any of claims 1-18, wherein the innermost cone (Cl) extends along only a part of the entire length of the at least one outer cone (C2). 21. The cone assembly according to any of claims 1-18, wherein the at least one outer cone (C2) extends along only a part of the entire length of the innermost cone (Cl). 22. The cone assembly according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the outward facing surface of the at least one outer cone (C2) is provided with a coating (14, 16). 23. The cone assembly according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the cones (Cl, C2) are attached to each other using an adhesive (28, 38). 24. The cone assembly according to any of the preceding claims, wherein a first end of the cone assembly has a first cross-sectional shape, wherein a second end of the cone assembly has a second cross-sectional shape being different from the first cross-sectional shape. 25. The cone assembly according to claim 24, wherein the first cross-sectional shape and/or the second cross-sectional shape is non-circular. 26. The cone assembly according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the cone assembly is provided with a longitudinal slit (60). 27. The cone assembly according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the first layer (Ll) of material of the outer cone (C2) has a fiber direction being different from the fiber direction of the material of the inner cone (Cl). 28. A method of manufacturing a cone assembly for furniture or interior accessories, the method comprising the steps of: providing a plurality of cones (Cl, C2), and arranging the plurality of cones (Cl, C2) in a linear array, whereby an inner cone (Cl) is received by at least one outer cone (C2), wherein the inner surface of the outer cone (C2) is substantially covering the entire outer surface of the inner cone (Cl). 29. The method according to claim 28, wherein the outer cone (C2) is made of a first layer (Ll) of material being different from the material of the inner cone (Cl). 30. The method according to claim 28 or 29, further comprising arranging at least one intermediate cone (C3) between the inner cone (Cl) and the outer cone (C2). 31. The method according to any of claims 28 - 30, wherein at least the outer cone (C2) is formed by arranging a sheet of the first layer (Ll) into a cone-shape. 32. The method according to claim 31, further comprising performing heat treatment of the sheet before or after arranging the sheet into the cone shape. 33. The method according to claim 31 or 32, further comprising performing flexing of the sheet before or after arranging the sheet into the cone shape. 34. The method according to any of claims 31-33, further comprising connecting the longitudinal edges (8) of the sheet to each other in order to form the cone-shape before the step of arranging the plurality of cones (Cl, C2, C3) in the linear array. 35. The method according to claim 34, wherein forming the sheets into separate cones (Cl, C2, C3) is performed by arranging the sheets in a mold (60). 36. The method according to claim 35, wherein arranging the sheets in the mold (60) is performed such that each cone (Cl, C2, C3) is provided with a circular cross-section along the entire length of the cone (Cl, C2, C3). 37. The method according to any of claims 31-36, wherein the sheet is formed by the first layer (Ll) being arranged adjacent to a second layer (L2) of material, the material of the second layer (L2) being different from the material of the first layer (Ll). 38. The method according to any of claims 28-37, further comprising pressing the linear array of cones (Cl, C2, C3) to a desired shape. 39. The method according to any of claims 28-38, further comprising arranging the fiber direction of the first layer (Ll) of material of the outer cone (C2) different from the fiber direction of the material of the inner cone (Cl) and/or different from the fiber direction of the material of the second layer (L2). 40. The method according to any of claims 28-39, further comprising closing at least one end of the cone assembly. 41. The method according to any of claims 28-40, wherein the inner cone (Cl) is hollow, and wherein the method further comprises arranging at least one support member (70) inside the inner cone (Cl). 42. The method according to claim 41, wherein the support member (70) comprises a through hole (72). 43. The method according to any of claims 28-42, further comprising filling the cone assembly with a filler material. 44. The method according to any of claims 28-43, further comprising attaching the cones (Cl, C2, C3) to each other by applying an adhesive to the outer surface of a cone (Cl, C3) prior to inserting the cone (Cl, C3) into an outer cone (C2, C3).
